benchmarks.name: Horizontal Scaling (Raft)
benchmarks.value: Full implementation — 13 modules, 4,896 LOC, 106 tests. Core consensus + advanced: topology governor, state publisher, entry dedup, chain groups, commit certificates
benchmarks.source: PR #218 (core) + PR #239 (entry dedup, depth-weighted election) + PR #247 (topology governor, state publisher). Core: leader election, log replication, compaction, persistence. Advanced: self-governing topology (auto-demotion/promotion, 8 tests), state-as-intelligence publisher (Raft telemetry as graph entities, 4 tests), entry deduplication (3 strategies including highest-confidence, 15 tests), chain groups (10 tests), commit certificates (13 tests), expected composition (13 tests), typed entries (12 tests).

benchmarks.name: Raft Membership Changes
benchmarks.value: build_membership_change() — ConfigChange entries for joint consensus
benchmarks.source: PR #218. WN-REBAL-03: build_membership_change() produces ConfigChange entries for Raft joint consensus (add/remove nodes). Tested as part of device test suite (10 tests).

benchmarks.name: Classification-Chain Partitioning
benchmarks.value: partition_router.rs (312 LOC, 5 tests) — governance-aware routing by LoR subtree for data locality. Multi-label entities route to multiple partitions
benchmarks.source: PR #218. Axiom 3 (Classification Chains as Graphs): multi-label classification means entities can be in multiple partitions. PartitionBoundary defines partition points in the chain (Axiom 4: boundaries are themselves governable entities). Router walks entity classification chain to find nearest boundary. Domain-agnostic: same logic for O&G (Basin > Field), Home IoT (Home > Kitchen), Health (Health > Activity). Cross-partition queries traverse normally (Axiom 8). Gap manifests filtered through partition scope. Enables geographic data locality (e.g., Alberta well data stays on Alberta regional node).

benchmarks.name: Federation / Multi-Instance
benchmarks.value: FWGA implemented — 28 sync tests, 6 manifest tests, 3 async worker tests
benchmarks.source: PRs #165, #169, #184. DeviceManifest: 5 tiers (Mothership/Regional/Desktop/Mobile/EdgeSensor) with LoR subtree scoping, classification gates, clearance max, section budget, confidence floor. sync_on_reconnect: 6-phase protocol (sweep, delta, reject expired, classify with real clearance from PREFIX_CLEARANCE index, apply with correct routing to PREFIX_SNAPSHOT/PREFIX_CONFLICT_QUEUE, gap manifest filtered through manifest). quorum-as-confidence: canonical_confidence_threshold f64 per type (Well 0.67, Property 0.67) replaces binary quorum. Background SyncWorker: tokio::spawn with reconnect Notify + 30s periodic fallback, failed sessions re-queue writes.

comparisons.source: LWW conflict resolution, no CBAC, no device hierarchy

sections.narrative: TrustDB's horizontal scaling story is now comprehensive with 106 replication tests across 13 modules. Single-node vertical scaling via B-epsilon tree, plus: (1) Raft consensus with advanced features — leader election, log replication, compaction, persistence, self-governing topology (auto-demotion/promotion based on health), state-as-intelligence publisher (Raft telemetry queryable as graph entities), entry deduplication (3 strategies including highest-confidence), chain groups, commit certificates, and typed entries. (2) Federation (FWGA) with 5-tier DeviceManifest hierarchy, clearance-aware sync, quorum-as-confidence thresholds, and governed conflict resolution. (3) Raft membership changes via joint consensus for dynamic cluster topology. (4) Classification-chain partitioning (partition_router.rs, 312 LOC) for governance-aware data locality — entities route based on their classification chain, not key ranges. No competitor combines self-governing Raft topology with CBAC-enforced federation and governance-aware partitioning. CockroachDB has Raft + range sharding but no epistemic semantics or self-governing topology. etcd has Raft but no multi-modal storage or auto-demotion. Remaining work: integration testing under real network partitions.

history.note: Chain A wiring + quorum-as-confidence. Federation moves from Designed to Implemented.
PR #218 — leader election, log replication, compaction, persistence + device lifecycle + rebalancing

history.value: Topology governor + state publisher + partition details
history.note: PR #247 — self-governing topology (8 tests), Raft telemetry as graph entities (4 tests), classification-chain routing (312 LOC, 5 tests). Total: 106 replication tests

sections.narrative: Data footprint is strong. WAF of 3.04 sits between LMDB's ideal 1x and RocksDB's compaction-heavy 10–30x. The B-ε tree offers better write amplification than LSM trees at the cost of slightly more reads. Governance metadata adds just 3.3% to storage — 49x cheaper than bolting governance onto a traditional database with extra tables and triggers. Insert scaling at 1.53x for 10x data is close to the B-ε theoretical optimum.

benchmarks.name: Online Device Add
benchmarks.source: PR #169. A new device creates a DeviceManifest (tier, LoR root, clearance max, classification filters), calls sync_with_manifest(), receives a filtered gap manifest (only data matching its scope), syncs, and is operational. No downtime for existing mesh members. Watermark starts at 0 for first sync, persists across sessions.

benchmarks.name: Online Device Remove
benchmarks.value: deregister_device() with data redistribution + connection graph tracking. 22 device tests across 3 modules
benchmarks.source: PR #218 + PR #247. Device registry (491 LOC, 9 tests), device manifest (304 LOC, 7 tests), device connection (358 LOC, 6 tests). deregister_device() marks device inactive, data redistribution triggered via sync_with_manifest on remaining nodes. Connection graph tracks device-to-device relationships, sync state, and reachability. Re-registration supported for returning devices (heartbeat resumes).

benchmarks.name: Automatic Rebalancing
benchmarks.value: Scope-change detection + auto-rebalance trigger — gap manifest filtered through updated manifest
benchmarks.source: PR #218. WN-REBAL-01: update_device_scope() detects ScopeChangeDelta (added/removed LoR subtrees). WN-REBAL-02: sync_with_manifest auto-triggered on scope change, gap manifest filtered through updated manifest — data flows automatically when a Regional server takes on a new province. Tests: scope update triggers rebalance.

benchmarks.name: Progressive Compaction
benchmarks.source: PR #218. Progressive compaction runs alongside rebalancing to reclaim space from migrated data. Integrated with maintenance module.

benchmarks.name: Read Scaling (flat curve)
benchmarks.source: B-epsilon tree O(log_B N) reads scale near-flat. Measured on SimulatedIo.

sections.narrative: Elasticity has transformed from the biggest scalability gap to fully implemented. All four dimensions are now green: (1) Online device add — zero-downtime via DeviceManifest + sync_with_manifest (scope-filtered gap manifest). (2) Online device remove — deregister_device() with data redistribution to remaining nodes and re-registration support. (3) Automatic rebalancing — scope-change detection (ScopeChangeDelta) triggers sync_with_manifest automatically when a device's LoR subtree changes (e.g., Regional server takes on a new province). (4) Progressive compaction — background space reclamation during rebalancing. Read scaling remains near-flat (20.2-22.5us for 10x data). Unlike CockroachDB (range-level) or Cassandra (token ring), TrustDB's elasticity is governance-aware — data routes based on classification chains and clearance levels, not just key ranges.

history.note: PR #218 — deregister_device(), scope-change rebalancing, progressive compaction. All 4 elasticity benchmarks green.

benchmarks.name: QUIC Transport vs gRPC
benchmarks.source: Spec Section 2.1. QUIC-native transport (quinn crate). Per-stream loss recovery — a dropped packet on the Health classification stream cannot stall the Asset stream. 0-RTT connection resumption for known FabricSession instances.

comparisons.value: TCP head-of-line blocking; all streams stall on one dropped packet
Request/response; no streaming; serialization overhead per hop

benchmarks.name: io_uring Zero-Copy Architecture
benchmarks.value: Zero heap allocations in hot path for 1 GB EpistemicBundle
benchmarks.source: Spec Section 2.2. Receive path: IORING_SETUP_SQPOLL + IORING_SETUP_IOPOLL — kernel writes QUIC stream data directly into pre-registered EpistemicBundle staging buffers. EntityFingerprint comparison + CABM merge priority + EpistemicState validation all run against staging buffer in-place. Send path: IORING_OP_SEND_ZC maps NVMe storage pages directly to NIC DMA buffers — server CPU does not touch Hologram payload data during transmission.

comparisons.value: Protobuf serialization + heap allocation per message

benchmarks.name: NUMA-Aware Morsel-Driven Execution
benchmarks.source: Spec Section 2.3. Graph workload divided into ~100K-row morsels. Rust threads pinned to NUMA-local cores. No cross-socket memory traffic. CBAC clearance evaluation + TDQP temporal decay + io_uring handoff all on NUMA-local memory.

comparisons.value: No NUMA awareness in standard implementation

benchmarks.name: GEMVT Causal Ordering
benchmarks.source: Spec Section 2.4. 64-bit Governance-Embedded MVCC Version Token: 12-bit Causal Fingerprint (O(1) EpochTable lookup, 0.024% false-positive), 8-bit Clearance Level (cryptographically bound to CBAC clearance), 36-bit Wall Time, 8-bit sub-millisecond counter. The 8-bit clearance field is a mathematical prerequisite for RDKC key derivation — unauthorized decryption is physically impossible.

comparisons.value: No clearance encoding, no causal fingerprint
No security field, no cryptographic clearance binding

benchmarks.name: RPC Surface Coverage
benchmarks.source: Implemented in trustdb/src/exchange/: OpenSession, UpdateSession, UpdateSpatialVolume, TraverseGraph, MaterializeRequest, PushDeltas, QueryGaps, SubscribeBeliefs, SpatialStream, ExchangeAtoms, ResolveVeiled. Files: quic_transport.rs, session.rs, rpc_surface.rs, hologram.rs, materialize.rs, epistemic_bundle.rs, belief_subscription.rs, differential.rs, spatial_session.rs, social_routing.rs, tci_materialization.rs, confidence_translation.rs, flight_adapter.rs, federated_intersection.rs + 22 supporting modules.

comparisons.value: 6 RPCs (no epistemic semantics, no spatial session, no belief subscription)
Protocol definition only — no epistemic state enforcement

benchmarks.name: Multi-Transport Adapters
benchmarks.value: BLE + HTTPS + UWB (Vision Pro) — 3 adapters, all tested
benchmarks.source: trustdb/src/exchange/transport_ble.rs, transport_https.rs, transport_uwb.rs. BLE: Watch/AirPods delta sync at low bandwidth. HTTPS: WiFi/LTE path. UWB: Vision Pro spatial sessions via NearbyInteraction ranging data as proxy for spatial volume scoping.

benchmarks.name: Hologram Protocol (Progressive Depth)
benchmarks.source: trustdb/src/exchange/hologram.rs + progressive_depth.rs. L1: structural skeleton (entity IDs, type labels, edge topology) — no payload. L2: L1 + confidence envelopes + epistemic state. L3: full materialization including provenance chains + TCI meta-edges. Device requests L1 first, then materializes deeper only for entities it needs. Reduces Watch battery wakeups vs CloudKit fetch-all-then-filter.

comparisons.value: Request-time field selection — entire payload resolved server-side
No progressive depth model — full payload always
Fetch-all-then-filter — full record payload regardless of needs

benchmarks.name: TCI Meta-Edge Materialization (Axiom 7)
benchmarks.source: trustdb/src/exchange/tci_materialization.rs. Lock-free AtomicU64 co-access counter tracks entity co-query patterns. When disparate entities are repeatedly co-queried, B-epsilon flush cycle materializes implicit correlations as explicit semantic meta-edges. Agents receive autonomously synthesized edges in EpistemicBundle without requesting them.

comparisons.value: No autonomous meta-edge synthesis — edges must be explicitly written

sections.narrative: Fabric Flight v1.0 (2026-04-03) is TrustDB's universal epistemic transport protocol — the wire layer for the governed mesh. It is not a replication format; it defines the vocabulary, wire primitives, and behavioral contracts for any node (TrustDB, DuckDB analytics tier, Swift in-memory graph, Python Parquet writer, federated partner) to participate in a governed epistemic mesh. The physical transport is QUIC-native (not HTTP/2), delivering ~25% throughput improvement and 28% p50 latency reduction vs Arrow Flight gRPC by eliminating TCP head-of-line blocking at the stream level. io_uring zero-copy achieves zero heap allocations in the 1GB EpistemicBundle hot path — NVMe pages map directly to NIC DMA buffers without userspace CPU touch. NUMA-aware morsel-driven execution achieves 80%+ parallel efficiency. The 64-bit GEMVT fuses HLC wall time with CBAC clearance — making unauthorized decryption physically impossible without the correct key, never derivable outside ARM TrustZone. The 11-RPC surface covers the full protocol lifecycle: session negotiation, graph traversal, differential sync (RFC 6902 JSON Patch), spatial session state, belief subscription (WAL-flush-triggered push), epistemic bundle exchange, and veiled entity resolution. Three transport adapters (BLE/HTTPS/UWB) enable Apple Watch, iPhone, and Vision Pro participation in the same governed mesh without any app changes.
